column2: Roll one time for each team - to see "Who has the day"
Roll 2 times for each team per game, one roll each per half of the game
The attack of a team is compared to the other teams defence for each roll.
Then all other bonuses apply, the end result difference decides what column to look in the Goal table sheet.
Goal table - Roll 2D6 - add the coach difference to the team with the better coach
Look at the roll and see how many goals were scored in the goal table.

column3: Attack

column2: 1. Each team rolls "Who has the day"

column3: Team A rolls 1
Team B rolls 0
Team A will get plus 1 on Attack, Defence and Stamina for the whole game
Team A has a base attack value of 5 - Team B has a base defence value of 4
So right now without any bonuses Team A has a result of 1 (5-4=1).
Team B has a base attack value of 6 - Team B has a base defence value of 2
So right now without any bonuses Team B has a result of 4 (6-2=4).
Team A has a Stamina of 7 and Team B has a stamina of 4, giving Team A bonus of 3.
Team A won the "Who has the day roll" by 1 increasing the difference to 4.
However in the first half the stamina difference is halved, (rounded down), resulting in 4 divided by 2 equals 2, no rounding needed.
So the result for Team A increase by 2 from 1 to 3.
So the result for Team B decrease by 2 from 4 to 2, since Team As defence get stronger from stamina.
Who has the Day

column4: Team A rolled 1 better on Who has the day so Attack/Defence increases by 1
Resulting in Team As value increase from 3 to 4.
Resulting in Team Bs value decreasing from 2 to 1.

column3: Home field advantage - Team A gets plus 1 to Attack and Defence

column4: So right now Team A has a result of 5 (4 previously +1 for home field)
So right now Team B has a result of 0 (1 previously, but Team A +1 for home field to defence decreasing the difference)

column3: Form Team A has form -0.25 and Team B has from 0,75, which is a difference in plus 1 to team B

column4: So right now Team A has a result of 4 (previously 5 but Team B decrease the difference with form by 1)
So right now Team A has a result of 1 (previously 0 Form increases the difference by 1)

column2: AFTER FIRST HALF THE STANDINGS ARE
Now you redo step 2-5 for the second half

column3: Team A has an attack value of 5 - Team B has a defence value of 4
So right now without any bonuses Team A has a result of 1.
Team B has an attack value of 6 - Team B has a defence value of 2
So right now without any bonuses Team B has a result of 4.
Team A has a Stamina of 7 and Team B has a stamina of 4, giving Team A bonus of 3.
Team A won the "Who has the day roll" by 1 increasing the difference to 4.
Since this is the second half of the game, the full amount of Stamina remains.
So right now Team A has a result of 5 (1+4=5)
The result for Team B has a result of 0 (4-4=0)

Resulting in Team Bs value decreasing from 0 to -1.

column2: THE FINAL SCORE OF THE GAME IS
In case it is a draw and the game doesn't allow the game to be a draw, for instance in a cup final, only one team can win.
Or if the combined result of two games showdown are equal, overtime and penalties will kick in.
Overtime - Roll again for a results using the same roll modifications used for the second half.
Penalties - Simply 50% either way, and simulating a penalty shootout score. There are coach attributes that can give advantage/disadvantage to one team.

Playoff can be either 1 game deciding the fate, like a final or qulification game. If it is a 1 game decider, the game is usually played on neutral ground. That is the decided by the creator if it should be.

But more commonly playoff will be, best out of a certain amount of games. For example best out of 3 games, then one team will have to win two games, iin these cases, the home game alternates between teams.
With the team who finished higer in the table or had more points, have the advantage of having one more home game.
Aggregated results, is almost always the combined result of 2 games between teams, one home and one away, and the team after both games with the best combined result win.
If the two teams after the end of the second game, has the same combined resutls, overtime and penalties should activate.

At creation of the League/Division, you decide at what point of the season that the bonus sets in, you set the amount of games remaining of the season.
Example, Motivation is set to activate 5 games before the end of season, any team in contention of any threshhold in the league gets the bonus.
